segments.text: This simple tap cost 22 lak rupees and it does not give water. When I first saw this at Viwork, I thought it was just a regular tap. But then I used the iPad
next to it and it poured the most amazing cold coffee. This is the top brewer. It gives you a perfect customized drink without a single
button. But experiencing it left me with a huge question. A thin metal tap cannot brew coffee. So where is the actual machine? To get this minimal look, they had to solve a huge design problem.
Normal coffee machines are loud, bulky,
and messy. So their secret was separating the interface from the engine. They took all the massive ugly parts, the bean grinder, the milk
frother and the cooler and buried them completely under the counter. But still 22 lakhs just to hire some wires and
tubes. Exactly. Elite workspaces aren't buying coffee. They are paying for zero clutter, absolute silence, and invisible design. What do you pay for it?
